perturbation.original_text: Royalty: In consideration of the rights granted to Licensee pursuant to this Amendment 2, Licensee shall pay to Fox, or such other party as Fox may designate in writing, a royalty in the following amount: (a) From the first unit sold, Fox shall earn a royalty at the rate of ***** percent (*****%) of Licensee's Gross Receipts (as defined in the Agreement) from Licensee's sale and distribution of the IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products. ("IA2 Audio and Video Royalty"). For the avoidance of doubt, Licensee may not recoup any portion of the Ice Age 2 Guarantee (as defined in Amendment 1) through its sale and distribution of the IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products.
perturbation.changed_text: Royalty: In consideration of the rights granted to Licensee pursuant to this Amendment 2, Licensee shall pay to Fox, or such other party as Fox may designate in writing, a royalty in the following amount: (a) From the first unit sold until 10,000 units are sold, Fox shall earn a royalty at the rate of ***** percent (*****%) of Licensee's Gross Receipts (as defined in the Agreement) from Licensee's sale and distribution of the IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products. After 10,000 units are sold, Fox shall earn a royalty at a rate of ***** percent (*****%) of Licensee's Gross Receipts. ("IA2 Audio and Video Royalty"). For the avoidance of doubt, Licensee may not recoup any portion of the Ice Age 2 Guarantee (as defined in Amendment 1) through its sale and distribution of the IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the royalty structure. Originally, Fox earned a consistent royalty rate from the first unit sold. Now, the royalty rate changes after 10,000 units, creating ambiguity. This contradicts the later statement in the same paragraph that "Licensee may not recoup any portion of the Ice Age 2 Guarantee (as defined in Amendment 1) through its sale and distribution of the IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products," because a lower royalty rate after 10,000 units effectively impacts the revenue flow affecting the recoupment ability and potentially making it difficult to know how to calculate it.
perturbation.location: B. IA 2 AUDIO AND VIDEO WIRELESS PRODUCTS ROYALTY: 1.
perturbation.contradicted_location: B. IA 2 AUDIO AND VIDEO WIRELESS PRODUCTS ROYALTY: 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Royalty: In consideration of the rights granted to Licensee pursuant to this Amendment 2, Licensee shall pay to Fox, or such other party as Fox may designate in writing, a royalty in the following amount: (a) From the first unit sold until 10,000 units are sold, Fox shall earn a royalty at the rate of ***** percent (*****%) of Licensee's Gross Receipts (as defined in the Agreement) from Licensee's sale and distribution of the IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products. After 10,000 units are sold, Fox shall earn a royalty at a rate of ***** percent (*****%) of Licensee's Gross Receipts. ("IA2 Audio and Video Royalty"). For the avoidance of doubt, Licensee may not recoup any portion of the Ice Age 2 Guarantee (as defined in Amendment 1) through its sale and distribution of the IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a tiered royalty structure, where the royalty rate changes after 10,000 units are sold. This directly contradicts the latter part of the paragraph stating, "Licensee may not recoup any portion of the Ice Age 2 Guarantee (as defined in Amendment 1) through its sale and distribution of the IA2 Audio and Video Wireless Products." The change in royalty rate after 10,000 units directly impacts the revenue stream and how the Ice Age 2 Guarantee recoupment is calculated, thus creating an in-text contradiction within the same paragraph. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections (or in this case, phrases within the same section) provide conflicting obligations or implications.

perturbation.original_text: All VGSL and other CSP payments remitted directly to Fox pursuant to the VGSL Agreement and other CSP agreements with respect to the IA Audio and Video Wireless Products shall be treated in the same manner as set forth in Paragraphs 7(b)(ii) and 7(b)(iii) of the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: All VGSL and other CSP payments remitted directly to Fox pursuant to the VGSL Agreement and other CSP agreements with respect to the IA Audio and Video Wireless Products shall be treated as additional gross receipts for calculation of royalties; however, Fox shall retain 25% of such payments for administrative costs.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. The original text stipulates that VGSL and CSP payments are treated according to Paragraphs 7(b)(ii) and 7(b)(iii) of the main Agreement, implying a specific calculation and distribution method. The modified text states that these payments are considered additional gross receipts for royalties, but then deducts 25% for administrative costs. This contradicts the original treatment method, as the 25% deduction is not specified or alluded to in Paragraphs 7(b)(ii) and 7(b)(iii). Therefore creating ambiguity.
perturbation.location: B. IA 2 AUDIO AND VIDEO WIRELESS PRODUCTS ROYALTY: 2.
perturbation.contradicted_location: B. IA 2 AUDIO AND VIDEO WIRELESS PRODUCTS ROYALTY: 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: VGSL and other CSP Accounting: All VGSL and other CSP payments remitted directly to Fox pursuant to the VGSL Agreement and other CSP agreements with respect to the IA Audio and Video Wireless Products shall be treated as additional gross receipts for calculation of royalties; however, Fox shall retain 25% of such payments for administrative costs. ***** The omitted portions of this exhibit have been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission pursuant to a request for confidential treatment under Rule 406 promulgated under the Securities Act of 1933. Source: GLU MOBILE INC, S-1/A, 3/19/2007
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text states that VGSL and CSP payments should be treated in accordance with paragraphs 7(b)(ii) and 7(b)(iii). However, the changed text says the payments will be treated as additional gross receipts for royalty calculation, with a 25% deduction for administrative costs. This directly contradicts the original instruction, as the 25% deduction is not present in paragraphs 7(b)(ii) and 7(b)(iii). Since the Contradicted Text is exactly the same as the Changed text, there is a strong in-text contradiction.
